title: Multi-Focus / Panorama Fusion (all-in-focus composite imaging)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical image-fusion literature (Burt & Adelson Laplacian pyramid, Szeliski panorama stitching, MFIF-GAN).

E.description: A sequence of K images {y_k} of the same scene is captured with varying focus distance z_k (multi-focus) or camera pose (panorama). Each y_k blurs the 3D scene x(u,v,z) through a depth-dependent PSF_k and an affine warp W_k. Fusion recovers an all-in-focus, globally aligned image X.
E.forward_model: y_k(u,v) = W_k{int_z PSF(u,v; z - z_k) * x(u,v,z) dz} + n_k(u,v)
E.world_state_x: All-in-focus composite X(u,v) and (optionally) a sparse depth map D(u,v)
E.observation_y: K focus-/pose-varied frames y_k in R^{H x W}, k = 1..K
physical_parameters_theta: Camera homographies {W_k} (panorama) or small translations (focus stack)
Circle-of-confusion diameter c(z; z_k) = A * |z - z_k| / z (A = aperture / z_k)
Sensor gain, read noise, motion jitter

W.regime: Well-conditioned when scenes are Lambertian, inter-frame parallax is small (pure rotation or small translation), and focus distances span the scene depth range. Degrades catastrophically for moving subjects (ghosting) and unconstrained parallax (depth ambiguity).

C.solver_class: Laplacian-pyramid fusion, gradient-based focus measure (SML, ML, tenengrad), Poisson-blend stitching, learned fusion (MFIF-GAN, U2Fusion), graph-cut seam selection
C.error_bound: ||X_hat - X||_2 <= C1 * sigma + C2 * parallax_displacement + C3 * misalignment_residual; seam artifacts quantified by local gradient magnitude at blend boundaries
C.complexity: O(K * H * W * log(max(H,W))) for pyramid; O(K * H * W) for gradient fusion
